revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 事代主神社 |画像 = [[画像:IMG_nitakubi.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町似首郷806 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|01|23.27|N|129|05|34.97|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 事代主神 外3柱 |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 文明15年（1483年）9月15日 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 =10月19日 |神事 = 上五島神楽（祇園際、例大祭） }}
'''事代主神社'''（ことしろぬしじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[新上五島町]]似首郷に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == [[事代主神]]、[[大国主神]]、[[スサノオ|素戔男神]]、[[大物主|琴平神]]の4柱を奉祀する。
祭神中の素戔男神は[[合祀]]された八坂神社の祭神である。
== 歴史 == 創建は[[文明]] 15年（[[1483年]]）9月15日。当郷は往古より性器崇拝があったとされ、郷内先磯辺の男性器を思わせる高く突出した立岩は神域とされ、社殿裏の恵美須神社には男性器の形をした石が祀ってある。 [[ファイル:IMG_nitakubi1.JPG|240px|thumb|恵美須神社]][[明和]]8年（[[1771年]]）に社殿を造営し、[[摂津]][[西ノ宮]]より[[西宮神社|恵美須神]]を勧進した。この頃より、社号を恵美須神社とするようになったと思われる。[[文化]]3年（[[1806年]]）に社殿が風波にさらわれ流出し、再建するも[[文久]]年間（[[1862年]]）火災により全焼した。 [[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）、社号を事代主神社に改称。同7年（[[1874年]]）5月、[[村社]]に列せられる。同41年（[[1908年]]）7月、郷内八坂神社を合祀。[[昭和]]8年（[[1933年]]）には2度目の火災により社殿が全焼した。翌9年（[[1934年]]）2月、神饌幣帛供進神社に指定された。
== 祭祀 ==
;主な祭礼・神事[[ファイル:IMG_ nitakubi2.JPG|240px|thumb|社殿]] *[[元旦祭]]（1月） *竜神祭（3月） *[[水神祭]]（4月） *夏越祭（6月） *市拝祭・[[祇園祭]]（7月） *[[例大祭]]（10月18、19日） *七五三祭（11月） *[[大祓式]]（12月）
== その他の神社 == その他の似首郷の神社に'''稲荷神社'''がある。
また、隣接する丸尾郷に'''[[丸尾神社]]'''が、小串郷に''' [[小串神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_maruo.JPG|丸尾神社（丸尾郷） 画像: IMG_2052.JPG|小串神社（小串郷）</gallery>
== 参考文献 == *『[[新魚目町]]郷土誌 昭和61年』新魚目町郷土誌編纂委員会 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部
{{DEFAULTSORT:ことしろぬししんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:長崎県の旧村社]] [[Category:新上五島町]] [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 事代主神社 |画像 = [[画像:IMG_nitakubi.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町似首郷806 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|01|23.27|N|129|05|34.97|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 事代主神 外3柱 |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 文明15年（1483年）9月15日 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 =10月19日 |神事 = 上五島神楽（祇園際、例大祭） }}
'''事代主神社'''（ことしろぬしじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[新上五島町]]似首郷に鎮座する[[神社]]である。

祭神中の素戔男神は[[合祀]]された八坂神社の祭神である。

== その他の神社 == その他の似首郷の神社に'''稲荷神社'''がある。
また、隣接する丸尾郷に'''[[丸尾神社]]'''が、小串郷に''' [[小串神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_maruo.JPG|丸尾神社（丸尾郷） 画像: IMG_2052.JPG|小串神社（小串郷）</gallery>
== 参考文献 == *『[[新魚目町]]郷土誌 昭和61年』新魚目町郷土誌編纂委員会 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部

{{神社 |名称 = 事代主神社 |画像 = [[画像:IMG_nitakubi.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町似首郷806 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|01|23.27|N|129|05|34.97|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 事代主神 外3柱 |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 文明15年（1483年）9月15日 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 =10月19日 |神事 = 上五島神楽（祇園祭、例大祭） }}
'''事代主神社'''（ことしろぬしじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[新上五島町]]似首郷に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == [[事代主神]]、[[大国主神]]、[[スサノオ|素戔男神]]、[[大物主|琴平神]]の4柱を奉祀する。

== 祭祀 == 祇園祭、10月18、19日の例祭には[[上五島神楽]]が奉納される。上五島神楽は国の[[選択無形民俗文化財]]に指定された[[五島神楽]]に含まれ、長崎県無形民俗文化財に単独で指定された。
;主な祭礼・神事[[ファイル:IMG_ nitakubi2.JPG|240px|thumb|社殿]] *[[元旦祭]]（1月） *竜神祭（3月） *[[水神祭]]（4月） *夏越祭（6月） *市拝祭・[[祇園祭]]（7月） *[[例大祭]]（10月18、19日） *七五三祭（11月） *[[大祓式]]（12月）
== その他の神社 == その他の似首郷の神社に'''稲荷神社'''がある。
また、隣接する丸尾郷に'''[[丸尾神社]]'''が、奈摩郷に'''[[政彦神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_maruo.JPG|丸尾神社（丸尾郷）</gallery>

== その他の神社 == その他の似首郷の神社に'''稲荷神社'''がある。
また、隣接する丸尾郷に'''[[丸尾神社]]'''が、奈摩郷に'''[[政彦神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_maruo.JPG|丸尾神社（丸尾郷） 画像: IMG_nama.JPG|政彦神社（奈摩郷）</gallery>
== 参考文献 == *『[[新魚目町]]郷土誌 昭和61年』新魚目町郷土誌編纂委員会 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部
{{DEFAULTSORT:ことしろぬししんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の旧村社]] [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 事代主神社 |画像 = [[画像:IMG_nitakubi.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町似首郷字堂脇806 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|01|08.4|N|129|05|29.8|E|scale:25000|name=事代主神社}} |祭神 = 事代主神 外3柱 |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 文明15年（1483年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月19日 |神事 = 上五島神楽（祇園祭、例大祭） }}
'''事代主神社'''（ことしろぬしじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[新上五島町]]似首（にたくび）郷に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == [[事代主神]]、[[大国主神]]、[[スサノオ|素戔男神]]、[[大物主|琴平神]]の4柱を奉祀する。
祭神中の素戔男神は[[合祀]]された八坂神社の祭神である。
== 歴史 == [[ファイル:IMG_ nitakubi2.JPG|170px|thumb|拝殿]] [[ファイル:IMG_nitakubi1.JPG|170px|thumb|恵美須神社]] 創建は[[文明 (日本)|文明]] 15年（[[1483年]]）9月15日。当郷は往古より[[性器]]崇拝があったとされ、郷内先磯辺の男性器を思わせる高く突出した立岩は神域とされており、社殿裏の恵美須神社には男性器の形をした石が祀ってある。
[[明和]]8年（[[1771年]]）に社殿を造営し、[[摂津]][[西宮神社]]より事代主神（[[えびす|恵美須神]]）を[[勧請]]した。この頃より、社号を恵美須神社とするようになったと思われる。[[文化 (元号)|文化]]3年（[[1806年]]）に社殿が風波にさらわれ流出し、再建するも[[文久]]元年（[[1862年]]）火災により全焼した。
[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）、社号を事代主神社に改称。同7年（[[1874年]]）5月、[[村社]]に列せられる。同41年（[[1908年]]）7月、郷内八坂神社を合祀。[[昭和]]8年（[[1933年]]）には2度目の火災により社殿が全焼した。翌9年（[[1934年]]）2月、[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定された。
== 祭祀 == 祇園祭、10月18、19日の例祭には[[上五島神楽]]が奉納される。上五島神楽は国の[[選択無形民俗文化財]]に指定された[[五島神楽]]に含まれ、長崎県無形民俗文化財に単独で指定された。
;主な祭礼・神事 *[[元旦祭]]（1月） *竜神祭（3月） *[[水神祭]]（4月） *夏越祭（6月） *市拝祭・[[祇園祭]]（7月） *[[例大祭]]（10月18、19日） *七五三祭（11月） *[[大祓式]]（12月）
== その他の神社 == その他の似首郷の神社に'''稲荷神社'''がある。
また、隣接する丸尾郷に'''[[丸尾神社]]'''が、奈摩郷に'''[[政彦神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_maruo.JPG|丸尾神社（丸尾郷） 画像: IMG_nama.JPG|政彦神社（奈摩郷） </gallery>
== 参考文献 == *『[[新魚目町]]郷土誌 昭和61年』新魚目町郷土誌編纂委員会 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部

'''事代主神社'''（ことしろぬしじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[新上五島町]]似首（にたくび）郷に鎮座する[[神社]]である。

[[明和]]8年（[[1771年]]）に社殿を造営し、[[摂津国|摂津]][[西宮神社]]より事代主神（[[えびす|恵美須神]]）を[[勧請]]した。この頃より、社号を恵美須神社とするようになったと思われる。[[文化 (元号)|文化]]3年（[[1806年]]）に社殿が風波にさらわれ流出し、再建するも[[文久]]元年（[[1862年]]）火災により全焼した。
[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）、社号を事代主神社に改称。同7年（[[1874年]]）5月、[[村社]]に列せられる。同41年（[[1908年]]）7月、郷内八坂神社を合祀。[[昭和]]8年（[[1933年]]）には2度目の火災により社殿が全焼した。翌9年（[[1934年]]）2月、[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定された。

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 事代主神社 |画像 = [[画像:IMG_nitakubi.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町似首郷字堂脇806 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|01|08.4|N|129|05|29.8|E|scale:25000|name=事代主神社}} |祭神 = 事代主神 外3柱 |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 文明15年（1483年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月19日 |神事 = 上五島神楽（祇園祭、例大祭） }}
'''事代主神社'''（ことしろぬしじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[新上五島町]]似首（にたくび）郷に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == [[事代主神]]、[[大国主神]]、[[スサノオ|素戔男神]]、[[大物主|琴平神]]の4柱を奉祀する。
祭神中の素戔男神は[[合祀]]された八坂神社の祭神である。
== 歴史 == [[ファイル:IMG_nitakubi2.JPG|170px|thumb|拝殿]] [[ファイル:IMG_nitakubi1.JPG|170px|thumb|恵美須神社]] 創建は[[文明 (日本)|文明]] 15年（[[1483年]]）9月15日。当郷は往古より[[性器]]崇拝があったとされ、郷内先磯辺の男性器を思わせる高く突出した立岩は神域とされており、社殿裏の恵美須神社には男性器の形をした石が祀ってある。
[[明和]]8年（[[1771年]]）に社殿を造営し、[[摂津国|摂津]][[西宮神社]]より事代主神（[[えびす|恵美須神]]）を[[勧請]]した。この頃より、社号を恵美須神社とするようになったと思われる。[[文化 (元号)|文化]]3年（[[1806年]]）に社殿が風波にさらわれ流出し、再建するも[[文久]]元年（[[1862年]]）火災により全焼した。
[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）、社号を事代主神社に改称。同7年（[[1874年]]）5月、[[村社]]に列せられる。同41年（[[1908年]]）7月、郷内八坂神社を合祀。[[昭和]]8年（[[1933年]]）には2度目の火災により社殿が全焼した。翌9年（[[1934年]]）2月、[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定された。
== 祭祀 == 祇園祭、10月18、19日の例祭には[[上五島神楽]]が奉納される。上五島神楽は1981年（昭和56年）に長崎県の[[無形民俗文化財]]に単独で指定されていたが、2002年（平成14年）2月12日には「'''五島神楽'''」の一つとして国の[[選択無形民俗文化財]]に選択され<ref>[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137114 文化遺産オンライン]</ref>、さらに2016年（平成28年）3月2日に国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された<ref>[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/459 長崎県の文化財（長崎県公式サイト）]</ref>。
;主な祭礼・神事 *[[元旦祭]]（1月） *竜神祭（3月） *[[水神祭]]（4月） *夏越祭（6月） *市拝祭・[[祇園祭]]（7月） *[[例大祭]]（10月18、19日） *七五三祭（11月） *[[大祓式]]（12月）
== その他の神社 == その他の似首郷の神社に'''稲荷神社'''がある。
また、隣接する丸尾郷に'''[[丸尾神社]]'''が、奈摩郷に'''[[政彦神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_maruo.JPG|丸尾神社（丸尾郷） 画像: IMG_nama.JPG|政彦神社（奈摩郷） </gallery>
==脚注== {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *『[[新魚目町]]郷土誌 昭和61年』新魚目町郷土誌編纂委員会 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部
{{DEFAULTSORT:ことしろぬししんしや}} [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] [[Category:えびす神社]] [[Category:長崎県の旧村社]] {{shinto-stub}}

== その他の神社 == その他の似首郷の神社に'''稲荷神社'''がある。
また、隣接する丸尾郷に'''[[丸尾神社]]'''が、奈摩郷に'''[[政彦神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_maruo.JPG|丸尾神社（丸尾郷） 画像: IMG_nama.JPG|政彦神社（奈摩郷） </gallery>

{{Shrine | name = {{ill|Kotoshironushi Shrine|ja|事代主神社}} | image = [[File:IMG_nitakubi.JPG|250px]] | location = {{ill|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, Nitakubi 806 | coordinates = {{coord|33|01|08.4|N|129|05|29.8|E|scale:25000|name=Kotoshironushi Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} and three other deities | status = Former village shrine | established = Bunmei 15 (1483) | main_hall = | festival = October 19 | rituals = {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽}} (Gion Festival, Annual Grand Festival) }}
'''{{ill|Kotoshironushi Shrine|ja|事代主神社}}''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nitakubi|ja|似首郷}}, {{ill|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities: * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔男神}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi (Konpira)|ja|大物主}} (worshiped as Konpira)
Among them, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔男神}} was enshrined following the merger with {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}}.
== History == [[File:IMG_nitakubi2.JPG|170px|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:IMG_nitakubi1.JPG|170px|thumb|Ebisu Shrine]]
The shrine was founded in Bunmei 15 (1483) on September 15. Historically, the region has practiced {{ill|phallus worship|ja|性器崇拝}}, with tall, protruding rocks resembling male genitalia considered sacred. Behind the shrine, {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社}} enshrines stones shaped like male genitalia.
In Meiwa 8 (1771), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} (worshipped as {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵美須神}}) was invited from {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|西宮神社}} in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. The shrine likely adopted the name {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社}} around this time. The shrine buildings were washed away by waves in Bunka 3 (1806), later rebuilt, but burned down in Bunkyū 1 (1862).
In Meiji 3 (1870), the shrine was renamed Kotoshironushi Shrine. In Meiji 7 (1874), it was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}. In Meiji 41 (1908), it merged with Yasaka Shrine. The shrine burned down again in Shōwa 8 (1933), but was designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社}} in Shōwa 9 (1934).
== Festivals == The {{ill|Gion Festival|ja|祇園祭}} and the annual festival on October 18–19 feature performances of {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽}}. This kagura was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 1981 and later as a national {{ill|Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財}} in 2002.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137114 Cultural Heritage Online]</ref> In 2016, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan.<ref>[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/459 Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Properties]</ref>
;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja|元旦祭}} (January) * Dragon God Festival (March) * {{ill|Water God Festival|ja|水神祭}} (April) * Nagoshi Festival (June) * {{ill|Gion Festival|ja|祇園祭}} (July) * {{ill|Annual Grand Festival|ja|例大祭}} (October 18–19) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja|七五三祭}} (November) * {{ill|Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja|大祓式}} (December)
== Nearby Shrines == Other shrines in Nitakubi include: * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}
Nearby districts also have shrines: * {{ill|Maruo Shrine|ja|丸尾神社}} in {{ill|Maruo|ja|丸尾郷}} * {{ill|Masahiko Shrine|ja|政彦神社}} in {{ill|Nama|ja|奈摩郷}}
<gallery> File:IMG_maruo.JPG|{{ill|Maruo Shrine|ja|丸尾神社}} (Maruo) File:IMG_nama.JPG|{{ill|Masahiko Shrine|ja|政彦神社}} (Nama) </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * ''Shingyome Town Local History'' (1986), Shingyome Town Local History Compilation Committee * ''Gotō Shrine Chronicle'' (1943 reprint), Jingikai South Matsura County

'''{{ill|Kotoshironushi Shrine|ja|事代主神社}}''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nitakubi|ja|似首郷}}, {{ill|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities: * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔男神}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} (worshiped as [[Konpira Gongen|Konpira]])
Among them, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔男神}} was enshrined following the merger with {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}}.
== History == [[File:IMG_nitakubi2.JPG|170px|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:IMG_nitakubi1.JPG|170px|thumb|Ebisu Shrine]]
The shrine was founded in Bunmei 15 (1483) on September 15. Historically, the region has practiced {{ill|phallus worship|ja|性器崇拝}}, with tall, protruding rocks resembling male genitalia considered sacred. Behind the shrine, {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社}} enshrines stones shaped like male genitalia.
In Meiwa 8 (1771), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} (worshipped as {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵美須神}}) was invited from {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|西宮神社}} in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. The shrine likely adopted the name {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社}} around this time. The shrine buildings were washed away by waves in Bunka 3 (1806), later rebuilt, but burned down in Bunkyū 1 (1862).
In Meiji 3 (1870), the shrine was renamed Kotoshironushi Shrine. In Meiji 7 (1874), it was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}. In Meiji 41 (1908), it merged with Yasaka Shrine. The shrine burned down again in Shōwa 8 (1933), but was designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社}} in Shōwa 9 (1934).
== Festivals == The {{ill|Gion Festival|ja|祇園祭}} and the annual festival on October 18–19 feature performances of {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽}}. This kagura was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 1981 and later as a national {{ill|Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財}} in 2002.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137114 Cultural Heritage Online]</ref> In 2016, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan.<ref>[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/459 Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Properties]</ref>
;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja|元旦祭}} (January) * Dragon God Festival (March) * {{ill|Water God Festival|ja|水神祭}} (April) * Nagoshi Festival (June) * {{ill|Gion Festival|ja|祇園祭}} (July) * {{ill|Annual Grand Festival|ja|例大祭}} (October 18–19) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja|七五三祭}} (November) * {{ill|Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja|大祓式}} (December)

{{Shrine | name = {{ill|Kotoshironushi Shrine|ja|事代主神社}} | image = [[File:IMG_nitakubi.JPG|250px]] | location = {{ill|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, Nitakubi 806 | coordinates = {{coord|33|01|08.4|N|129|05|29.8|E|scale:25000|name=Kotoshironushi Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} and three other deities | status = Former village shrine | established = Bunmei 15 (1483) | main_hall = | festival = October 19 | rituals = {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽}} (Gion Festival, Annual Grand Festival) }}
'''{{ill|Kotoshironushi Shrine|ja|事代主神社}}''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nitakubi|ja|似首郷}}, {{ill|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities: * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔男神}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} (worshiped as [[Konpira Gongen|Konpira]])
Among them, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔男神}} was enshrined following the merger with {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}}.
== History == [[File:IMG_nitakubi2.JPG|170px|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:IMG_nitakubi1.JPG|170px|thumb|Ebisu Shrine]]
The shrine was founded in Bunmei 15 (1483) on September 15. Historically, the region has practiced {{ill|phallus worship|ja|性器崇拝}}, with tall, protruding rocks resembling male genitalia considered sacred. Behind the shrine, {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社}} enshrines stones shaped like male genitalia.
In Meiwa 8 (1771), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} (worshipped as {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵美須神}}) was invited from {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|西宮神社}} in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. The shrine likely adopted the name {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社}} around this time. The shrine buildings were washed away by waves in Bunka 3 (1806), later rebuilt, but burned down in Bunkyū 1 (1862).
In Meiji 3 (1870), the shrine was renamed Kotoshironushi Shrine. In Meiji 7 (1874), it was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}. In Meiji 41 (1908), it merged with Yasaka Shrine. The shrine burned down again in Shōwa 8 (1933), but was designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社}} in Shōwa 9 (1934).
== Festivals == The {{ill|Gion Festival|ja|祇園祭}} and the annual festival on October 18–19 feature performances of {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽}}. This kagura was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 1981 and later as a national {{ill|Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財}} in 2002.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137114 Cultural Heritage Online]</ref> In 2016, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of Japan.<ref>[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/459 Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Properties]</ref>
;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja|元旦祭}} (January) * Dragon God Festival (March) * {{ill|Water God Festival|ja|水神祭}} (April) * Nagoshi Festival (June) * {{ill|Gion Festival|ja|祇園祭}} (July) * {{ill|Annual Grand Festival|ja|例大祭}} (October 18–19) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja|七五三祭}} (November) * {{ill|Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja|大祓式}} (December)
== Nearby Shrines == Other shrines in Nitakubi include: * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}
Nearby districts also have shrines: * {{ill|Maruo Shrine|ja|丸尾神社}} in {{ill|Maruo|ja|丸尾郷}} * {{ill|Masahiko Shrine|ja|政彦神社}} in {{ill|Nama|ja|奈摩郷}}

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 事代主神社 |画像 = [[画像:IMG_nitakubi.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町似首郷字堂脇806 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|01|08.4|N|129|05|29.8|E|scale:25000|name=事代主神社}} |祭神 = 事代主神 外3柱 |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 文明15年（1483年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月19日 |神事 = 上五島神楽（祇園祭、例大祭） }}
'''事代主神社'''（ことしろぬしじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[新上五島町]]似首（にたくび）郷に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == [[事代主神]]、[[大国主神]]、[[スサノオ|素戔男神]]、[[大物主|琴平神]]の4柱を奉祀する。
祭神中の素戔男神は[[合祀]]された八坂神社の祭神である。
== 歴史 == [[ファイル:IMG_nitakubi2.JPG|170px|thumb|拝殿]] [[ファイル:IMG_nitakubi1.JPG|170px|thumb|恵美須神社]] 創建は[[文明 (日本)|文明]] 15年（[[1483年]]）9月15日。当郷は往古より[[性器]]崇拝があったとされ、郷内先磯辺の男性器を思わせる高く突出した立岩は神域とされており、社殿裏の恵美須神社には男性器の形をした石が祀ってある。
[[明和]]8年（[[1771年]]）に社殿を造営し、[[摂津国|摂津]][[西宮神社]]より事代主神（[[えびす|恵美須神]]）を[[勧請]]した。この頃より、社号を恵美須神社とするようになったと思われる。[[文化 (元号)|文化]]3年（[[1806年]]）に社殿が風波にさらわれ流出し、再建するも[[文久]]元年（[[1862年]]）火災により全焼した。
[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）、社号を事代主神社に改称。同7年（[[1874年]]）5月、[[村社]]に列せられる。同41年（[[1908年]]）7月、郷内八坂神社を合祀。[[昭和]]8年（[[1933年]]）には2度目の火災により社殿が全焼した。翌9年（[[1934年]]）2月、[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定された。
== 祭祀 == 祇園祭、10月18、19日の例祭には[[上五島神楽]]が奉納される。上五島神楽は1981年（昭和56年）に長崎県の[[無形民俗文化財]]に単独で指定されていたが、2002年（平成14年）2月12日には「'''五島神楽'''」の一つとして国の[[選択無形民俗文化財]]に選択され<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137114 文化遺産オンライン]</ref>、さらに2016年（平成28年）3月2日に国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された<ref>[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/459 長崎県の文化財（長崎県公式サイト）]</ref>。
;主な祭礼・神事 *[[元旦祭]]（1月） *竜神祭（3月） *[[水神祭]]（4月） *夏越祭（6月） *市拝祭・[[祇園祭]]（7月） *[[例大祭]]（10月18、19日） *七五三祭（11月） *[[大祓式]]（12月）
== その他の神社 == その他の似首郷の神社に'''稲荷神社'''がある。
また、隣接する丸尾郷に'''[[丸尾神社]]'''が、奈摩郷に'''[[政彦神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_maruo.JPG|丸尾神社（丸尾郷） 画像: IMG_nama.JPG|政彦神社（奈摩郷） </gallery>
==脚注== {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *『[[新魚目町]]郷土誌 昭和61年』新魚目町郷土誌編纂委員会 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部
{{DEFAULTSORT:ことしろぬししんしや}} [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] [[Category:えびす神社]] [[Category:長崎県の旧村社]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[事代主神社 (新上五島町)]] to [[Kotoshironushi Shrine (Shinkamigoto Town)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kotoshironushi-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:事代主神社 (新上五島町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11371126]] Wikidata name: Kotoshironushi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:19, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotoshironushi Shrine | image = File:IMG nitakubi.JPG | location = {{ill|Shinkamigoto|qid=Q1206790}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.019|N|129.091611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (worshiped as [[Konpira Gongen|Konpira]]) | status = Former Village shrine | established = Bunmei 15 (1483) | main_hall = | festival = October 19 | rituals = {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Gion Festival, Annual Grand Festival) }}
'''{{ill|Kotoshironushi Shrine|ja|事代主神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi Shrine|qq=}}''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nitakubi|ja|似首郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nitakubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]].
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities: * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (worshiped as [[Konpira Gongen|Konpira]])
Among them, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was enshrined following the merger with [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]].
== History == [[File:IMG_nitakubi2.JPG|170px|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:IMG_nitakubi1.JPG|170px|thumb|Ebisu Shrine]]
The shrine was founded in Bunmei 15 (1483) on September 15. Historically, the region has practiced {{ill|phallus worship|ja|性器崇拝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phallus worship|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 生殖器崇拝|1=sex worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11072719}}, with tall, protruding rocks resembling male genitalia considered sacred. Behind the shrine, {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} enshrines stones shaped like male genitalia.
In Meiwa 8 (1771), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (worshipped as {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵美須神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was invited from [[:en:Nishinomiya Shrine|Nishinomiya Shrine]] in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]]. The shrine likely adopted the name {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} around this time. The shrine buildings were washed away by waves in Bunka 3 (1806), later rebuilt, but burned down in Bunkyū 1 (1862).
In Meiji 3 (1870), the shrine was renamed Kotoshironushi Shrine. In Meiji 7 (1874), it was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In Meiji 41 (1908), it merged with Yasaka Shrine. The shrine burned down again in Shōwa 8 (1933), but was designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Shōwa 9 (1934).
== Festivals == The [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] and the annual festival on October 18–19 feature performances of {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This kagura was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} in 1981 and later as a national {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} in 2002.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137114 Cultural Heritage Online]</ref> In 2016, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan.<ref>[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/459 Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Properties]</ref>
;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja|元旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January) * Dragon God Festival (March) * {{ill|Suijin Festival|ja|水神祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Festival|qq=}} (April) * Nagoshi Festival (June) * [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] (July) * {{ill|Reisai|fr|Reisai|lt=Annual Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (October 18–19) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja|七五三祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November) * {{ill|Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja|大祓式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December)
== Nearby Shrines == Other shrines in Nitakubi include: * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]
Nearby districts also have shrines: * {{ill|Maruo Shrine|ja|丸尾神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo Shrine|qq=|1=Maruo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368122}} in {{ill|Maruo|ja|丸尾郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Masahiko Shrine|ja|政彦神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Shrine|qq=|1=Masahiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499110}} in {{ill|Nama|ja|奈摩郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}
<gallery> File:IMG_maruo.JPG|{{ill|Maruo Shrine|ja|丸尾神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo Shrine|qq=}} (Maruo) File:IMG_nama.JPG|{{ill|Masahiko Shrine|ja|政彦神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Shrine|qq=}} (Nama) </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * ''Shingyome Town Local History'' (1986), Shingyome Town Local History Compilation Committee * ''Gotō Shrine Chronicle'' (1943 reprint), Jingikai South Matsura County

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kotoshironushi-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:事代主神社 (新上五島町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11371126]] Wikidata name: Kotoshironushi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:19, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotoshironushi Shrine | image = File:IMG nitakubi.JPG | location = {{ill|Shinkamigoto|qid=Q1206790}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.019|N|129.091611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (worshiped as [[Konpira Gongen|Konpira]]) | status = Former Village shrine | established = Bunmei 15 (1483) | main_hall = | festival = October 19 | rituals = {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Gion Festival, Annual Grand Festival) }}
'''{{ill|Kotoshironushi Shrine|ja|事代主神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi Shrine|qq=}}''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nitakubi|ja|似首郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nitakubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]].
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities: * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (worshiped as [[Konpira Gongen|Konpira]])
Among them, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was enshrined following the merger with [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]].
== History == [[File:IMG_nitakubi2.JPG|170px|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:IMG_nitakubi1.JPG|170px|thumb|Ebisu Shrine]]
The shrine was founded in Bunmei 15 (1483) on September 15. Historically, the region has practiced {{ill|phallus worship|ja|性器崇拝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phallus worship|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 生殖器崇拝|1=sex worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11072719}}, with tall, protruding rocks resembling male genitalia considered sacred. Behind the shrine, {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} enshrines stones shaped like male genitalia.
In Meiwa 8 (1771), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (worshipped as {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵美須神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was invited from [[:en:Nishinomiya Shrine|Nishinomiya Shrine]] in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]]. The shrine likely adopted the name {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} around this time. The shrine buildings were washed away by waves in Bunka 3 (1806), later rebuilt, but burned down in Bunkyū 1 (1862).
In Meiji 3 (1870), the shrine was renamed Kotoshironushi Shrine. In Meiji 7 (1874), it was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In Meiji 41 (1908), it merged with Yasaka Shrine. The shrine burned down again in Shōwa 8 (1933), but was designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Shōwa 9 (1934).
== Festivals == The [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] and the annual festival on October 18–19 feature performances of {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This kagura was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} in 1981 and later as a national {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} in 2002.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137114 Cultural Heritage Online]</ref> In 2016, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan.<ref>[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/459 Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Properties]</ref>
;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja|元旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January) * Dragon God Festival (March) * {{ill|Suijin Festival|ja|水神祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Festival|qq=}} (April) * Nagoshi Festival (June) * [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] (July) * {{ill|Reisai|fr|Reisai|lt=Annual Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (October 18–19) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja|七五三祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November) * {{ill|Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja|大祓式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December)
== Nearby Shrines == Other shrines in Nitakubi include: * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]
Nearby districts also have shrines: * {{ill|Maruo Shrine|ja|丸尾神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo Shrine|qq=|1=Maruo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368122}} in {{ill|Maruo|ja|丸尾郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Masahiko Shrine|ja|政彦神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Shrine|qq=|1=Masahiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499110}} in {{ill|Nama|ja|奈摩郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}
<gallery> File:IMG_maruo.JPG|{{ill|Maruo Shrine|ja|丸尾神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo Shrine|qq=}} (Maruo) File:IMG_nama.JPG|{{ill|Masahiko Shrine|ja|政彦神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Shrine|qq=}} (Nama) </gallery>

'''{{ill|Kotoshironushi Shrine|ja|事代主神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi Shrine|qq=}}''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nitakubi|ja|似首郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nitakubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]].
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities: * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (worshiped as [[Konpira Gongen|Konpira]])
Among them, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was enshrined following the merger with [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]].
== History == [[File:IMG_nitakubi2.JPG|170px|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:IMG_nitakubi1.JPG|170px|thumb|Ebisu Shrine]]
The shrine was founded in Bunmei 15 (1483) on September 15. Historically, the region has practiced {{ill|phallus worship|ja|性器崇拝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phallus worship|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 生殖器崇拝|1=sex worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11072719}}, with tall, protruding rocks resembling male genitalia considered sacred. Behind the shrine, {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} enshrines stones shaped like male genitalia.
In Meiwa 8 (1771), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (worshipped as {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵美須神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was invited from [[:en:Nishinomiya Shrine|Nishinomiya Shrine]] in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]]. The shrine likely adopted the name {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} around this time. The shrine buildings were washed away by waves in Bunka 3 (1806), later rebuilt, but burned down in Bunkyū 1 (1862).
In Meiji 3 (1870), the shrine was renamed Kotoshironushi Shrine. In Meiji 7 (1874), it was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In Meiji 41 (1908), it merged with Yasaka Shrine. The shrine burned down again in Shōwa 8 (1933), but was designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Shōwa 9 (1934).
== Festivals == The [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] and the annual festival on October 18–19 feature performances of {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This kagura was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} in 1981 and later as a national {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} in 2002.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137114 Cultural Heritage Online]</ref> In 2016, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan.<ref>[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/459 Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Properties]</ref>
;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja|元旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January) * Dragon God Festival (March) * {{ill|Suijin Festival|ja|水神祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Festival|qq=}} (April) * Nagoshi Festival (June) * [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] (July) * {{ill|Reisai|fr|Reisai|lt=Annual Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (October 18–19) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja|七五三祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November) * {{ill|Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja|大祓式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December)
== Nearby Shrines == Other shrines in Nitakubi include: * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]
Nearby districts also have shrines: * {{ill|Maruo Shrine|ja|丸尾神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo Shrine|qq=|1=Maruo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368122}} in {{ill|Maruo|ja|丸尾郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Masahiko Shrine|ja|政彦神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Shrine|qq=|1=Masahiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499110}} in {{ill|Nama|ja|奈摩郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}
<gallery> File:IMG_maruo.JPG|{{ill|Maruo Shrine|ja|丸尾神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo Shrine|qq=}} (Maruo) File:IMG_nama.JPG|{{ill|Masahiko Shrine|ja|政彦神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Shrine|qq=}} (Nama) </gallery>

'''{{ill|Kotoshironushi Shrine|ja|事代主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi Shrine|qq=}}''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nitakubi|ja|似首郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nitakubi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]].
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities: * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (worshiped as [[Konpira Gongen|Konpira]])
Among them, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was enshrined following the merger with [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]].
== History == [[File:IMG_nitakubi2.JPG|170px|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:IMG_nitakubi1.JPG|170px|thumb|Ebisu Shrine]]
The shrine was founded in Bunmei 15 (1483) on September 15. Historically, the region has practiced {{ill|phallus worship|ja|性器崇拝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phallus worship|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 生殖器崇拝|1=sex worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11072719}}, with tall, protruding rocks resembling male genitalia considered sacred. Behind the shrine, {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} enshrines stones shaped like male genitalia.
In Meiwa 8 (1771), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (worshipped as {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵美須神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was invited from [[:en:Nishinomiya Shrine|Nishinomiya Shrine]] in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]]. The shrine likely adopted the name {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} around this time. The shrine buildings were washed away by waves in Bunka 3 (1806), later rebuilt, but burned down in Bunkyū 1 (1862).
In Meiji 3 (1870), the shrine was renamed Kotoshironushi Shrine. In Meiji 7 (1874), it was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In Meiji 41 (1908), it merged with Yasaka Shrine. The shrine burned down again in Shōwa 8 (1933), but was designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Shōwa 9 (1934).
== Festivals == The [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] and the annual festival on October 18–19 feature performances of {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This kagura was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} in 1981 and later as a national {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} in 2002.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137114 Cultural Heritage Online]</ref> In 2016, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan.<ref>[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/459 Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Properties]</ref>
;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January) * Dragon God Festival (March) * {{ill|Suijin Festival|ja|水神祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Festival|qq=}} (April) * Nagoshi Festival (June) * [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] (July) * {{ill|Reisai|fr|Reisai|lt=Annual Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (October 18–19) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja|七五三祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November) * {{ill|Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December)
== Nearby Shrines == Other shrines in Nitakubi include: * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]
Nearby districts also have shrines: * {{ill|Maruo Shrine|ja|丸尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo Shrine|1=Maruo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368122}} in {{ill|Maruo|ja|丸尾郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Masahiko Shrine|ja|政彦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Shrine|1=Masahiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499110}} in {{ill|Nama|ja|奈摩郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}
<gallery> File:IMG_maruo.JPG|{{ill|Maruo Shrine|ja|丸尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo Shrine|qq=}} (Maruo) File:IMG_nama.JPG|{{ill|Masahiko Shrine|ja|政彦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Shrine|qq=}} (Nama) </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * ''Shingyome Town Local History'' (1986), Shingyome Town Local History Compilation Committee * ''Gotō Shrine Chronicle'' (1943 reprint), Jingikai South Matsura County

Among them, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was enshrined following the merger with [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]].
== History == [[File:IMG_nitakubi2.JPG|170px|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:IMG_nitakubi1.JPG|170px|thumb|Ebisu Shrine]]
The shrine was founded in Bunmei 15 (1483) on September 15. Historically, the region has practiced {{ill|phallus worship|ja|性器崇拝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phallus worship|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 生殖器崇拝|1=sex worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11072719}}, with tall, protruding rocks resembling male genitalia considered sacred. Behind the shrine, {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} enshrines stones shaped like male genitalia.

The shrine likely adopted the name {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} around this time. The shrine buildings were washed away by waves in Bunka 3 (1806), later rebuilt, but burned down in Bunkyū 1 (1862).
In Meiji 3 (1870), the shrine was renamed Kotoshironushi Shrine. In Meiji 7 (1874), it was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In Meiji 41 (1908), it merged with Yasaka Shrine. The shrine burned down again in Shōwa 8 (1933), but was designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Shōwa 9 (1934).
== Festivals == The [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] and the annual festival on October 18–19 feature performances of {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This kagura was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} in 1981 and later as a national {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} in 2002.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137114 Cultural Heritage Online]</ref> In 2016, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan.<ref>[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/459 Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Properties]</ref>
;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January) * Dragon God Festival (March) * {{ill|Suijin Festival|ja|水神祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Festival|qq=}} (April) * Nagoshi Festival (June) * [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] (July) * {{ill|Reisai|fr|Reisai|lt=Annual Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (October 18–19) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja|七五三祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November) * {{ill|Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December)

== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities: * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (worshiped as [[Konpira Gongen|Konpira]])
Among them, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was enshrined following the merger with [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]].
== History == [[File:IMG_nitakubi2.JPG|170px|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:IMG_nitakubi1.JPG|170px|thumb|Ebisu Shrine]]
The shrine was founded in Bunmei 15 (1483) on September 15. Historically, the region has practiced {{ill|phallus worship|ja|性器崇拝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phallus worship|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 生殖器崇拝|1=sex worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11072719}}, with tall, protruding rocks resembling male genitalia considered sacred. Behind the shrine, {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} enshrines stones shaped like male genitalia.
In Meiwa 8 (1771), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (worshipped as {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵美須神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was invited from [[:en:Nishinomiya Shrine|Nishinomiya Shrine]] in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]]. The shrine likely adopted the name {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} around this time. The shrine buildings were washed away by waves in Bunka 3 (1806), later rebuilt, but burned down in Bunkyū 1 (1862).
In Meiji 3 (1870), the shrine was renamed Kotoshironushi Shrine. In Meiji 7 (1874), it was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In Meiji 41 (1908), it merged with Yasaka Shrine. The shrine burned down again in Shōwa 8 (1933), but was designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Shōwa 9 (1934).
== Festivals == The [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] and the annual festival on October 18–19 feature performances of {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This kagura was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} in 1981 and later as a national {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} in 2002.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137114 Cultural Heritage Online]</ref> In 2016, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan.<ref>[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/459 Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Properties]</ref>
;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January) * Dragon God Festival (March) * {{ill|Suijin Festival|ja|水神祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Festival|qq=}} (April) * Nagoshi Festival (June) * [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] (July) * {{ill|Reisai|fr|Reisai|lt=Annual Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (October 18–19) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja|七五三祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November) * {{ill|Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December)
== Nearby Shrines == Other shrines in Nitakubi include: * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]
Nearby districts also have shrines: * {{ill|Maruo Shrine|ja|丸尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo Shrine|1=Maruo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368122}} in {{ill|Maruo|ja|丸尾郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Masahiko Shrine|ja|政彦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Shrine|1=Masahiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499110}} in {{ill|Nama|ja|奈摩郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}
<gallery> File:IMG_maruo.JPG|{{ill|Maruo Shrine|ja|丸尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo Shrine|qq=}} (Maruo) File:IMG_nama.JPG|{{ill|Masahiko Shrine|ja|政彦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Shrine|qq=}} (Nama) </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * ''Shingyome Town Local History'' (1986), Shingyome Town Local History Compilation Committee * ''Gotō Shrine Chronicle'' (1943 reprint), Jingikai South Matsura County

'''{{ill|Kotoshironushi Shrine|ja|事代主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi Shrine|qq=}}''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nitakubi|ja|似首郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nitakubi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]].
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities: * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (worshiped as [[Konpira Gongen|Konpira]])
Among them, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was enshrined following the merger with [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]].
== History == [[File:IMG_nitakubi2.JPG|170px|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:IMG_nitakubi1.JPG|170px|thumb|Ebisu Shrine]]
The shrine was founded in Bunmei 15 (1483) on September 15. Historically, the region has practiced {{ill|phallus worship|ja|性器崇拝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phallus worship|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 生殖器崇拝|1=sex worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11072719}}, with tall, protruding rocks resembling male genitalia considered sacred. Behind the shrine, {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} enshrines stones shaped like male genitalia.
In Meiwa 8 (1771), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (worshipped as {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵美須神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was invited from [[:en:Nishinomiya Shrine|Nishinomiya Shrine]] in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]]. The shrine likely adopted the name {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} around this time. The shrine buildings were washed away by waves in Bunka 3 (1806), later rebuilt, but burned down in Bunkyū 1 (1862).
In Meiji 3 (1870), the shrine was renamed Kotoshironushi Shrine. In Meiji 7 (1874), it was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In Meiji 41 (1908), it merged with Yasaka Shrine. The shrine burned down again in Shōwa 8 (1933), but was designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Shōwa 9 (1934).
== Festivals == The [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] and the annual festival on October 18–19 feature performances of {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This kagura was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} in 1981 and later as a national {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} in 2002.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137114 Cultural Heritage Online]</ref> In 2016, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan.<ref>[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/459 Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Properties]</ref>
;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January) * Dragon God Festival (March) * {{ill|Suijin Festival|ja|水神祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Festival|qq=}} (April) * Nagoshi Festival (June) * [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] (July) * {{ill|Reisai|fr|Reisai|lt=Annual Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (October 18–19) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja|七五三祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November) * {{ill|Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December)
== Nearby Shrines == Other shrines in Nitakubi include: * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]
Nearby districts also have shrines: * {{ill|Maruo Shrine|ja|丸尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo Shrine|1=Maruo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368122}} in {{ill|Maruo|ja|丸尾郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Masahiko Shrine|ja|政彦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Shrine|1=Masahiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499110}} in {{ill|Nama|ja|奈摩郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}
<gallery> File:IMG_maruo.JPG|{{ill|Maruo Shrine|ja|丸尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo Shrine|qq=}} (Maruo) File:IMG_nama.JPG|{{ill|Masahiko Shrine|ja|政彦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Shrine|qq=}} (Nama) </gallery>

== Festivals == The [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] and the annual festival on October 18–19 feature performances of {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This kagura was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} in 1981 and later as a national {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} in 2002.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137114 Cultural Heritage Online]</ref> In 2016, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan.<ref>[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/459 Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Properties]</ref>

'''{{ill|Kotoshironushi Shrine|ja|事代主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi Shrine|qq=}}''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nitakubi|ja|似首郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nitakubi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]].
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities: * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (worshiped as [[Konpira Gongen|Konpira]])
Among them, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was enshrined following the merger with [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]].
== History == [[File:IMG_nitakubi2.JPG|170px|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:IMG_nitakubi1.JPG|170px|thumb|Ebisu Shrine]]
The shrine was founded in Bunmei 15 (1483) on September 15. Historically, the region has practiced {{ill|phallus worship|ja|性器崇拝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phallus worship|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 生殖器崇拝|1=sex worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11072719}}, with tall, protruding rocks resembling male genitalia considered sacred. Behind the shrine, {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} enshrines stones shaped like male genitalia.
In Meiwa 8 (1771), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (worshipped as {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵美須神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was invited from [[:en:Nishinomiya Shrine|Nishinomiya Shrine]] in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]]. The shrine likely adopted the name {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} around this time. The shrine buildings were washed away by waves in Bunka 3 (1806), later rebuilt, but burned down in Bunkyū 1 (1862).
In Meiji 3 (1870), the shrine was renamed Kotoshironushi Shrine. In Meiji 7 (1874), it was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In Meiji 41 (1908), it merged with Yasaka Shrine. The shrine burned down again in Shōwa 8 (1933), but was designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Shōwa 9 (1934).
== Festivals == The [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] and the annual festival on October 18–19 feature performances of {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This kagura was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} in 1981 and later as a national {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} in 2002.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137114 Cultural Heritage Online]</ref> In 2016, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan.<ref>[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/459 Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Properties]</ref>
;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January) * Dragon God Festival (March) * {{ill|Suijin Festival|ja|水神祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Festival|qq=}} (April) * Nagoshi Festival (June) * [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] (July) * {{ill|Reisai|fr|Reisai|lt=Annual Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (October 18–19) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja|七五三祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November) * {{ill|Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December)

== Nearby Shrines == Other shrines in Nitakubi include: * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]
Nearby districts also have shrines: * {{ill|Maruo Shrine|ja|丸尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo Shrine|1=Maruo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368122}} in {{ill|Maruo|ja|丸尾郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Masahiko Shrine|ja|政彦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Shrine|1=Masahiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499110}} in {{ill|Nama|ja|奈摩郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}
