[[Category:Shinto shrines in Shinkamigoto]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Kotoshironushi-jinja (Shinkamigoto)}} [[ja:事代主神社 (新上五島町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11371126}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kotoshironushi-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:事代主神社 (新上五島町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11371126]] Wikidata name: Kotoshironushi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:19, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotoshironushi Shrine | image = File:IMG nitakubi.JPG | location = {{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.019|N|129.091611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (worshiped as [[Konpira Gongen|Konpira]]) | status = Former Village shrine | established = Bunmei 15 (1483) | main_hall = | festival = October 19 | rituals = {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Gion Festival, Annual Grand Festival) }}
'''{{ill|Kotoshironushi Shrine|ja|事代主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi Shrine|qq=}}''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nitakubi|ja|似首郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nitakubi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]].
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities: * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (worshiped as [[Konpira Gongen|Konpira]])
Among them, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was enshrined following the merger with [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]].
== History == [[File:IMG_nitakubi2.JPG|170px|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:IMG_nitakubi1.JPG|170px|thumb|Ebisu Shrine]]
The shrine was founded in Bunmei 15 (1483) on September 15. Historically, the region has practiced {{ill|phallus worship|ja|性器崇拝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phallus worship|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 生殖器崇拝|1=sex worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11072719}}, with tall, protruding rocks resembling male genitalia considered sacred. Behind the shrine, {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} enshrines stones shaped like male genitalia.
In Meiwa 8 (1771), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (worshipped as {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵美須神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was invited from [[:en:Nishinomiya Shrine|Nishinomiya Shrine]] in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]]. The shrine likely adopted the name {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} around this time. The shrine buildings were washed away by waves in Bunka 3 (1806), later rebuilt, but burned down in Bunkyū 1 (1862).
In Meiji 3 (1870), the shrine was renamed Kotoshironushi Shrine. In Meiji 7 (1874), it was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In Meiji 41 (1908), it merged with Yasaka Shrine. The shrine burned down again in Shōwa 8 (1933), but was designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Shōwa 9 (1934).
== Festivals == The [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] and the annual festival on October 18–19 feature performances of {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This kagura was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} in 1981 and later as a national {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} in 2002.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137114 Cultural Heritage Online]</ref> In 2016, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan.<ref>[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/459 Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Properties]</ref>
;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January) * Dragon God Festival (March) * {{ill|Suijin Festival|ja|水神祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Festival|qq=}} (April) * Nagoshi Festival (June) * [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] (July) * {{ill|Reisai|fr|Reisai|lt=Annual Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (October 18–19) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja|七五三祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November) * {{ill|Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December)
== Nearby Shrines == Other shrines in Nitakubi include: * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]
Nearby districts also have shrines: * {{ill|Maruo Shrine|ja|丸尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo Shrine|1=Maruo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368122}} in {{ill|Maruo|ja|丸尾郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Masahiko Shrine|ja|政彦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Shrine|1=Masahiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499110}} in {{ill|Nama|ja|奈摩郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}
<gallery> File:IMG_maruo.JPG|{{ill|Maruo Shrine|ja|丸尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo Shrine|qq=}} (Maruo) File:IMG_nama.JPG|{{ill|Masahiko Shrine|ja|政彦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Shrine|qq=}} (Nama) </gallery>

'''{{ill|Kotoshironushi Shrine|ja|事代主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi Shrine|qq=}}''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nitakubi|ja|似首郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nitakubi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]].
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities: * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (worshiped as [[Konpira Gongen|Konpira]])
Among them, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was enshrined following the merger with [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]].
== History == [[File:IMG_nitakubi2.JPG|170px|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:IMG_nitakubi1.JPG|170px|thumb|Ebisu Shrine]]
The shrine was founded in Bunmei 15 (1483) on September 15. Historically, the region has practiced {{ill|phallus worship|ja|性器崇拝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phallus worship|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 生殖器崇拝|1=sex worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11072719}}, with tall, protruding rocks resembling male genitalia considered sacred. Behind the shrine, {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} enshrines stones shaped like male genitalia.
In Meiwa 8 (1771), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (worshipped as {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵美須神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was invited from [[:en:Nishinomiya Shrine|Nishinomiya Shrine]] in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]]. The shrine likely adopted the name {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} around this time. The shrine buildings were washed away by waves in Bunka 3 (1806), later rebuilt, but burned down in Bunkyū 1 (1862).
In Meiji 3 (1870), the shrine was renamed Kotoshironushi Shrine. In Meiji 7 (1874), it was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In Meiji 41 (1908), it merged with Yasaka Shrine. The shrine burned down again in Shōwa 8 (1933), but was designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Shōwa 9 (1934).
== Festivals == The [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] and the annual festival on October 18–19 feature performances of {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This kagura was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} in 1981 and later as a national {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} in 2002.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137114 Cultural Heritage Online]</ref> In 2016, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan.<ref>[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/459 Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Properties]</ref>
;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January) * Dragon God Festival (March) * {{ill|Suijin Festival|ja|水神祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Festival|qq=}} (April) * Nagoshi Festival (June) * [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] (July) * {{ill|Reisai|fr|Reisai|lt=Annual Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (October 18–19) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja|七五三祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November) * {{ill|Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December)
== Nearby Shrines == Other shrines in Nitakubi include: * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]
Nearby districts also have shrines: * {{ill|Maruo Shrine|ja|丸尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo Shrine|1=Maruo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368122}} in {{ill|Maruo|ja|丸尾郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Masahiko Shrine|ja|政彦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Shrine|1=Masahiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499110}} in {{ill|Nama|ja|奈摩郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kotoshironushi-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:事代主神社 (新上五島町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11371126]] Wikidata name: Kotoshironushi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:19, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotoshironushi Shrine | image = File:IMG nitakubi.JPG | location = {{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.019|N|129.091611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (worshiped as [[Konpira Gongen|Konpira]]) | status = Former Village shrine | established = Bunmei 15 (1483) | main_hall = | festival = October 19 | rituals = {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Gion Festival, Annual Grand Festival) }}
'''{{ill|Kotoshironushi Shrine|ja|事代主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi Shrine|qq=}}''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nitakubi|ja|似首郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nitakubi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]].
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities: * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (worshiped as [[Konpira Gongen|Konpira]])
Among them, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was enshrined following the merger with [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]].
== History == [[File:IMG_nitakubi2.JPG|170px|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:IMG_nitakubi1.JPG|170px|thumb|Ebisu Shrine]]
The shrine was founded in Bunmei 15 (1483) on September 15. Historically, the region has practiced {{ill|phallus worship|ja|性器崇拝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phallus worship|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 生殖器崇拝|1=sex worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11072719}}, with tall, protruding rocks resembling male genitalia considered sacred. Behind the shrine, {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} enshrines stones shaped like male genitalia.
In Meiwa 8 (1771), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (worshipped as {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵美須神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was invited from [[:en:Nishinomiya Shrine|Nishinomiya Shrine]] in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]]. The shrine likely adopted the name {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} around this time. The shrine buildings were washed away by waves in Bunka 3 (1806), later rebuilt, but burned down in Bunkyū 1 (1862).
In Meiji 3 (1870), the shrine was renamed Kotoshironushi Shrine. In Meiji 7 (1874), it was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In Meiji 41 (1908), it merged with Yasaka Shrine. The shrine burned down again in Shōwa 8 (1933), but was designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Shōwa 9 (1934).
== Festivals == The [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] and the annual festival on October 18–19 feature performances of {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This kagura was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} in 1981 and later as a national {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} in 2002.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137114 Cultural Heritage Online]</ref> In 2016, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan.<ref>[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/459 Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Properties]</ref>
;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January) * Dragon God Festival (March) * {{ill|Suijin Festival|ja|水神祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Festival|qq=}} (April) * Nagoshi Festival (June) * [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] (July) * {{ill|Reisai|fr|Reisai|lt=Annual Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (October 18–19) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja|七五三祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November) * {{ill|Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December)
== Nearby Shrines == Other shrines in Nitakubi include: * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]
Nearby districts also have shrines: * {{ill|Maruo Shrine|ja|丸尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo Shrine|1=Maruo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368122}} in {{ill|Maruo|ja|丸尾郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Masahiko Shrine|ja|政彦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Shrine|1=Masahiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499110}} in {{ill|Nama|ja|奈摩郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}
<gallery> File:IMG_maruo.JPG|{{ill|Maruo Shrine|ja|丸尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo Shrine|qq=}} (Maruo) File:IMG_nama.JPG|{{ill|Masahiko Shrine|ja|政彦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Shrine|qq=}} (Nama) </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * ''Shingyome Town Local History'' (1986), Shingyome Town Local History Compilation Committee * ''Gotō Shrine Chronicle'' (1943 reprint), Jingikai South Matsura County

== Festivals == The [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] and the annual festival on October 18–19 feature performances of {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This kagura was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} in 1981 and later as a national {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} in 2002.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137114 Cultural Heritage Online]</ref> In 2016, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan.<ref>[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/459 Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Properties]</ref>
;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January) * Dragon God Festival (March) * {{ill|Suijin Festival|ja|水神祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Festival|qq=}} (April) * Nagoshi Festival (June) * [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] (July) * {{ill|Reisai|fr|Reisai|lt=Annual Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (October 18–19) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja|七五三祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November) * {{ill|Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December)
== Nearby Shrines == Other shrines in Nitakubi include: * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]
Nearby districts also have shrines: * {{ill|Maruo Shrine|ja|丸尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo Shrine|1=Maruo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368122}} in {{ill|Maruo|ja|丸尾郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Masahiko Shrine|ja|政彦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Shrine|1=Masahiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499110}} in {{ill|Nama|ja|奈摩郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}

'''{{ill|Kotoshironushi Shrine|ja|事代主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi Shrine|qq=}}''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nitakubi|ja|似首郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nitakubi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]].
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities: * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (worshiped as [[Konpira Gongen|Konpira]])
Among them, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was enshrined following the merger with [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]].
== History == [[File:IMG_nitakubi2.JPG|170px|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:IMG_nitakubi1.JPG|170px|thumb|Ebisu Shrine]]
The shrine was founded in Bunmei 15 (1483) on September 15. Historically, the region has practiced {{ill|phallus worship|ja|性器崇拝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phallus worship|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 生殖器崇拝|1=sex worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11072719}}, with tall, protruding rocks resembling male genitalia considered sacred. Behind the shrine, {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} enshrines stones shaped like male genitalia.
In Meiwa 8 (1771), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (worshipped as {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵美須神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was invited from [[:en:Nishinomiya Shrine|Nishinomiya Shrine]] in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]]. The shrine likely adopted the name {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} around this time. The shrine buildings were washed away by waves in Bunka 3 (1806), later rebuilt, but burned down in Bunkyū 1 (1862).
In Meiji 3 (1870), the shrine was renamed Kotoshironushi Shrine. In Meiji 7 (1874), it was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In Meiji 41 (1908), it merged with Yasaka Shrine. The shrine burned down again in Shōwa 8 (1933), but was designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Shōwa 9 (1934).
== Festivals == The [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] and the annual festival on October 18–19 feature performances of {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This kagura was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} in 1981 and later as a national {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} in 2002.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137114 Cultural Heritage Online]</ref> In 2016, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan.<ref>[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/459 Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Properties]</ref>
;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January) * Dragon God Festival (March) * {{ill|Suijin Festival|ja|水神祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Festival|qq=}} (April) * Nagoshi Festival (June) * [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] (July) * {{ill|Reisai|fr|Reisai|lt=Annual Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (October 18–19) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja|七五三祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November) * {{ill|Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December)
== Nearby Shrines == Other shrines in Nitakubi include: * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]
Nearby districts also have shrines: * {{ill|Maruo Shrine|ja|丸尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo Shrine|1=Maruo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368122}} in {{ill|Maruo|ja|丸尾郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Masahiko Shrine|ja|政彦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Shrine|1=Masahiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499110}} in {{ill|Nama|ja|奈摩郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}
<gallery> File:IMG_maruo.JPG|{{ill|Maruo Shrine|ja|丸尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo Shrine|qq=}} (Maruo) File:IMG_nama.JPG|{{ill|Masahiko Shrine|ja|政彦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Shrine|qq=}} (Nama) </gallery>

The shrine likely adopted the name {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} around this time. The shrine buildings were washed away by waves in Bunka 3 (1806), later rebuilt, but burned down in Bunkyū 1 (1862).
In Meiji 3 (1870), the shrine was renamed Kotoshironushi Shrine. In Meiji 7 (1874), it was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In Meiji 41 (1908), it merged with Yasaka Shrine. The shrine burned down again in Shōwa 8 (1933), but was designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Shōwa 9 (1934).

This kagura was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} in 1981 and later as a national {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} in 2002.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137114 Cultural Heritage Online]</ref> In 2016, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan.<ref>[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/459 Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Properties]</ref>

== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities: * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (worshiped as [[Konpira Gongen|Konpira]])
Among them, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was enshrined following the merger with [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]].
== History == [[File:IMG_nitakubi2.JPG|170px|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:IMG_nitakubi1.JPG|170px|thumb|Ebisu Shrine]]
The shrine was founded in Bunmei 15 (1483) on September 15. Historically, the region has practiced {{ill|phallus worship|ja|性器崇拝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phallus worship|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 生殖器崇拝|1=sex worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11072719}}, with tall, protruding rocks resembling male genitalia considered sacred. Behind the shrine, {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} enshrines stones shaped like male genitalia.

The shrine likely adopted the name {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} around this time. The shrine buildings were washed away by waves in Bunka 3 (1806), later rebuilt, but burned down in Bunkyū 1 (1862).
In Meiji 3 (1870), the shrine was renamed Kotoshironushi Shrine. In Meiji 7 (1874), it was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In Meiji 41 (1908), it merged with Yasaka Shrine. The shrine burned down again in Shōwa 8 (1933), but was designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Shōwa 9 (1934).
== Festivals == The [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] and the annual festival on October 18–19 feature performances of {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This kagura was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} in 1981 and later as a national {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} in 2002.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137114 Cultural Heritage Online]</ref> In 2016, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan.<ref>[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/459 Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Properties]</ref>
;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January) * Dragon God Festival (March) * {{ill|Suijin Festival|ja|水神祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Festival|qq=}} (April) * Nagoshi Festival (June) * [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] (July) * {{ill|Reisai|fr|Reisai|lt=Annual Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (October 18–19) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja|七五三祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November) * {{ill|Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December)
== Nearby Shrines == Other shrines in Nitakubi include: * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kotoshironushi-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:事代主神社 (新上五島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kotoshironushi-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:事代主神社 (新上五島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kotoshironushi-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:事代主神社 (新上五島町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11371126]] Wikidata name: Kotoshironushi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:19, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotoshironushi Shrine | image = File:IMG nitakubi.JPG | location = {{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.019|N|129.091611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (worshiped as [[Konpira Gongen|Konpira]]) | status = Former Village shrine | established = Bunmei 15 (1483) | main_hall = | festival = October 19 | rituals = {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Gion Festival, Annual Grand Festival) }}
'''{{ill|Kotoshironushi Shrine|ja|事代主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi Shrine|qq=}}''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nitakubi|ja|似首郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nitakubi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]].
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities: * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] (worshiped as [[Konpira Gongen|Konpira]])
Among them, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was enshrined following the merger with [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]].
== History == [[File:IMG_nitakubi2.JPG|170px|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:IMG_nitakubi1.JPG|170px|thumb|Ebisu Shrine]]
The shrine was founded in Bunmei 15 (1483) on September 15. Historically, the region has practiced {{ill|phallus worship|ja|性器崇拝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phallus worship|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 生殖器崇拝|1=sex worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11072719}}, with tall, protruding rocks resembling male genitalia considered sacred. Behind the shrine, {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} enshrines stones shaped like male genitalia.
In Meiwa 8 (1771), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (worshipped as {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵美須神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was invited from [[:en:Nishinomiya Shrine|Nishinomiya Shrine]] in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]]. The shrine likely adopted the name {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} around this time. The shrine buildings were washed away by waves in Bunka 3 (1806), later rebuilt, but burned down in Bunkyū 1 (1862).
In Meiji 3 (1870), the shrine was renamed Kotoshironushi Shrine. In Meiji 7 (1874), it was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In Meiji 41 (1908), it merged with Yasaka Shrine. The shrine burned down again in Shōwa 8 (1933), but was designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Shōwa 9 (1934).
== Festivals == The [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] and the annual festival on October 18–19 feature performances of {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This kagura was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} in 1981 and later as a national {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} in 2002.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137114 Cultural Heritage Online]</ref> In 2016, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan.<ref>[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/459 Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Properties]</ref>
;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January) * Dragon God Festival (March) * {{ill|Suijin Festival|ja|水神祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Festival|qq=}} (April) * Nagoshi Festival (June) * [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] (July) * {{ill|Reisai|fr|Reisai|lt=Annual Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (October 18–19) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja|七五三祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November) * {{ill|Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December)
== Nearby Shrines == Other shrines in Nitakubi include: * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]

== Festivals == The {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Festival}} and the annual festival on October 18–19 feature performances of {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This kagura was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} in 1981 and later as a national {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} in 2002.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137114 Cultural Heritage Online]</ref> In 2016, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan.<ref>[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/459 Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Properties]</ref>
;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January) * Dragon God Festival (March) * {{ill|Suijin Festival|ja|水神祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Festival|qq=}} (April) * Nagoshi Festival (June) * {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Festival}} (July) * {{ill|Reisai|fr|Reisai|lt=Annual Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (October 18–19) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja|七五三祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November) * {{ill|Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December)
== Nearby Shrines == Other shrines in Nitakubi include: * {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}}
Nearby districts also have shrines: * {{ill|Maruo Shrine|ja|丸尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo Shrine|1=Maruo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368122}} in {{ill|Maruo|ja|丸尾郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Masahiko Shrine|ja|政彦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Shrine|1=Masahiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499110}} in {{ill|Nama|ja|奈摩郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kotoshironushi-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:事代主神社 (新上五島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kotoshironushi-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:事代主神社 (新上五島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kotoshironushi-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:事代主神社 (新上五島町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11371126]] Wikidata name: Kotoshironushi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:19, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotoshironushi Shrine | image = File:IMG nitakubi.JPG | location = {{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.019|N|129.091611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi}} (worshiped as [[Konpira Gongen|Konpira]]) | status = Former Village shrine | established = Bunmei 15 (1483) | main_hall = | festival = October 19 | rituals = {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Gion Festival, Annual Grand Festival) }}
'''{{ill|Kotoshironushi Shrine|ja|事代主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi Shrine|qq=}}''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Nitakubi|ja|似首郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nitakubi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shin-Kamigotō|en|Shin-Kamigotō|WD=Q1206790|lt=Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture}}.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities: * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi}} (worshiped as [[Konpira Gongen|Konpira]])
Among them, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was enshrined following the merger with {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine}}.
== History == [[File:IMG_nitakubi2.JPG|170px|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:IMG_nitakubi1.JPG|170px|thumb|Ebisu Shrine]]
The shrine was founded in Bunmei 15 (1483) on September 15. Historically, the region has practiced {{ill|phallus worship|ja|性器崇拝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phallus worship|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 生殖器崇拝|1=sex worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11072719}}, with tall, protruding rocks resembling male genitalia considered sacred. Behind the shrine, {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} enshrines stones shaped like male genitalia.
In Meiwa 8 (1771), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (worshipped as {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵美須神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was invited from {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|en|Nishinomiya Shrine|WD=Q705297|lt=Nishinomiya Shrine}} in {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province}}. The shrine likely adopted the name {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} around this time. The shrine buildings were washed away by waves in Bunka 3 (1806), later rebuilt, but burned down in Bunkyū 1 (1862).
In Meiji 3 (1870), the shrine was renamed Kotoshironushi Shrine. In Meiji 7 (1874), it was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In Meiji 41 (1908), it merged with Yasaka Shrine. The shrine burned down again in Shōwa 8 (1933), but was designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Shōwa 9 (1934).
== Festivals == The {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Festival}} and the annual festival on October 18–19 feature performances of {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This kagura was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} in 1981 and later as a national {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} in 2002.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137114 Cultural Heritage Online]</ref> In 2016, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan.<ref>[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/459 Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Properties]</ref>
;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January) * Dragon God Festival (March) * {{ill|Suijin Festival|ja|水神祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Festival|qq=}} (April) * Nagoshi Festival (June) * {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Festival}} (July) * {{ill|Reisai|fr|Reisai|lt=Annual Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (October 18–19) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja|七五三祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November) * {{ill|Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December)
== Nearby Shrines == Other shrines in Nitakubi include: * {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}}
Nearby districts also have shrines: * {{ill|Maruo Shrine|ja|丸尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo Shrine|1=Maruo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368122}} in {{ill|Maruo|ja|丸尾郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Masahiko Shrine|ja|政彦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Shrine|1=Masahiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499110}} in {{ill|Nama|ja|奈摩郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}
<gallery> File:IMG_maruo.JPG|{{ill|Maruo Shrine|ja|丸尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo Shrine|qq=}} (Maruo) File:IMG_nama.JPG|{{ill|Masahiko Shrine|ja|政彦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Shrine|qq=}} (Nama) </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}

== Festivals == The {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Festival}} and the annual festival on October 18–19 feature performances of {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This kagura was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} in 1981 and later as a national {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} in 2002.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137114 Cultural Heritage Online]</ref> In 2016, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan.<ref>[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/459 Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Properties]</ref>
;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January) * Dragon God Festival (March) * {{ill|Suijin Festival|ja|水神祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Festival|qq=}} (April) * Nagoshi Festival (June) * {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Festival}} (July) * {{ill|Reisai|fr|Reisai|lt=Annual Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (October 18–19) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja|七五三祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November) * {{ill|Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December)
== Nearby Shrines == Other shrines in Nitakubi include: * {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}}
Nearby districts also have shrines: * {{ill|Maruo Shrine|ja|丸尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo Shrine|1=Maruo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368122}} in {{ill|Maruo|ja|丸尾郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Masahiko Shrine|ja|政彦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Shrine|1=Masahiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499110}} in {{ill|Nama|ja|奈摩郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kotoshironushi-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:事代主神社 (新上五島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kotoshironushi-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:事代主神社 (新上五島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kotoshironushi-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:事代主神社 (新上五島町)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotoshironushi Shrine | image = File:IMG nitakubi.JPG | location = {{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.019|N|129.091611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi}} (worshiped as [[Konpira Gongen|Konpira]]) | status = Former Village shrine | established = Bunmei 15 (1483) | main_hall = | festival = October 19 | rituals = {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Gion Festival, Annual Grand Festival) }}
'''{{ill|Kotoshironushi Shrine|ja|事代主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi Shrine|qq=}}''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Nitakubi|ja|似首郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nitakubi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shin-Kamigotō|en|Shin-Kamigotō|WD=Q1206790|lt=Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture}}.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities: * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi}} (worshiped as [[Konpira Gongen|Konpira]])
Among them, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was enshrined following the merger with {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine}}.
== History == [[File:IMG_nitakubi2.JPG|170px|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:IMG_nitakubi1.JPG|170px|thumb|Ebisu Shrine]]
The shrine was founded in Bunmei 15 (1483) on September 15. Historically, the region has practiced {{ill|phallus worship|ja|性器崇拝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phallus worship|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 生殖器崇拝|1=sex worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11072719}}, with tall, protruding rocks resembling male genitalia considered sacred. Behind the shrine, {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} enshrines stones shaped like male genitalia.
In Meiwa 8 (1771), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (worshipped as {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵美須神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was invited from {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|en|Nishinomiya Shrine|WD=Q705297|lt=Nishinomiya Shrine}} in {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province}}. The shrine likely adopted the name {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} around this time. The shrine buildings were washed away by waves in Bunka 3 (1806), later rebuilt, but burned down in Bunkyū 1 (1862).
In Meiji 3 (1870), the shrine was renamed Kotoshironushi Shrine. In Meiji 7 (1874), it was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In Meiji 41 (1908), it merged with Yasaka Shrine. The shrine burned down again in Shōwa 8 (1933), but was designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Shōwa 9 (1934).
== Festivals == The {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Festival}} and the annual festival on October 18–19 feature performances of {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This kagura was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} in 1981 and later as a national {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} in 2002.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137114 Cultural Heritage Online]</ref> In 2016, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan.<ref>[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/459 Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Properties]</ref>
;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January) * Dragon God Festival (March) * {{ill|Suijin Festival|ja|水神祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Festival|qq=}} (April) * Nagoshi Festival (June) * {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Festival}} (July) * {{ill|Reisai|fr|Reisai|lt=Annual Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (October 18–19) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja|七五三祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November) * {{ill|Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December)
== Nearby Shrines == Other shrines in Nitakubi include: * {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}}
Nearby districts also have shrines: * {{ill|Maruo Shrine|ja|丸尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo Shrine|1=Maruo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368122}} in {{ill|Maruo|ja|丸尾郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Masahiko Shrine|ja|政彦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Shrine|1=Masahiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499110}} in {{ill|Nama|ja|奈摩郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}
<gallery> File:IMG_maruo.JPG|{{ill|Maruo Shrine|ja|丸尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo Shrine|qq=}} (Maruo) File:IMG_nama.JPG|{{ill|Masahiko Shrine|ja|政彦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Shrine|qq=}} (Nama) </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * ''Shingyome Town Local History'' (1986), Shingyome Town Local History Compilation Committee * ''Gotō Shrine Chronicle'' (1943 reprint), Jingikai South Matsura County

This kagura was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} in 1981 and later as a national {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} in 2002.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137114 Cultural Heritage Online]</ref> In 2016, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan.<ref>[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/459 Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Properties]</ref>

'''{{ill|Kotoshironushi Shrine|ja|事代主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi Shrine|qq=}}''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Nitakubi|ja|似首郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nitakubi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shin-Kamigotō|en|Shin-Kamigotō|WD=Q1206790|lt=Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture}}.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities: * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi}} (worshiped as [[Konpira Gongen|Konpira]])
Among them, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was enshrined following the merger with {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine}}.
== History == [[File:IMG_nitakubi2.JPG|170px|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:IMG_nitakubi1.JPG|170px|thumb|Ebisu Shrine]]
The shrine was founded in Bunmei 15 (1483) on September 15. Historically, the region has practiced {{ill|phallus worship|ja|性器崇拝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phallus worship|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 生殖器崇拝|1=sex worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11072719}}, with tall, protruding rocks resembling male genitalia considered sacred. Behind the shrine, {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} enshrines stones shaped like male genitalia.
In Meiwa 8 (1771), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (worshipped as {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵美須神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was invited from {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|en|Nishinomiya Shrine|WD=Q705297|lt=Nishinomiya Shrine}} in {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province}}. The shrine likely adopted the name {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵美須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} around this time. The shrine buildings were washed away by waves in Bunka 3 (1806), later rebuilt, but burned down in Bunkyū 1 (1862).
In Meiji 3 (1870), the shrine was renamed Kotoshironushi Shrine. In Meiji 7 (1874), it was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In Meiji 41 (1908), it merged with Yasaka Shrine. The shrine burned down again in Shōwa 8 (1933), but was designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Shōwa 9 (1934).
== Festivals == The {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Festival}} and the annual festival on October 18–19 feature performances of {{ill|Kamigoto Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This kagura was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} in 1981 and later as a national {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} in 2002.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137114 Cultural Heritage Online]</ref> In 2016, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan.<ref>[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/459 Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Properties]</ref>

This kagura was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nagasaki Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} in 1981 and later as a national {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} in 2002.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137114 Cultural Heritage Online]</ref> In 2016, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of Japan.<ref>[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/459 Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Properties]</ref>
;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gantan-sai (New Year's Festival)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} (January) * Dragon God Festival (March) * {{ill|Suijin Festival|ja|水神祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Festival|qq=}} (April) * Nagoshi Festival (June) * {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Festival}} (July) * {{ill|Reisai|fr|Reisai|lt=Annual Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (October 18–19) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja|七五三祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November) * {{ill|Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=ōharae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11261910}} (December)
== Nearby Shrines == Other shrines in Nitakubi include: * {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}}
Nearby districts also have shrines: * {{ill|Maruo Shrine|ja|丸尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo Shrine|1=Maruo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368122}} in {{ill|Maruo|ja|丸尾郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Masahiko Shrine|ja|政彦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Shrine|1=Masahiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499110}} in {{ill|Nama|ja|奈摩郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}
